warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.limit: Verification required via GSFA online database
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the Preamble for interpreting the data. The database is regularly updated.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.limit: Maximum levels (MLs) or guideline levels must be adhered to. Verification required via relevant Codex texts/standards.
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: Contaminants are substances not intentionally added to food. Codex MLs are established to protect human health and are kept as low as possible based on scientific evidence. The General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) is a key reference.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Maximum levels (MLs) must be adhered to. Verification required via relevant Codex texts/standards.
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: Codex has established MLs for specific contaminants like mycotoxins, metals (arsenic, lead, mercury), and radionuclides. These MLs ensure food does not contain contaminants at levels threatening human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: Codex has established 17 (MLs) for contaminants including mycotoxins (poisonous funghi produced by certain chemical compounds), metals (such as arsenic, lead and mercury) and radionuclides

rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: The Union list of food additives and their conditions of use is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.limit: Verification required against Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: This regulation lays down food safety criteria and process hygiene criteria for specific foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for pre-packaged foodstuffs
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة
rules.notes: This technical regulation covers general labelling requirements for pre-packaged food products, including information such as ingredients, nutritional facts, and origin.
rules.evidenceQuote: بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.parameter: Halal food general requirements (for products making a Halal claim)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال
rules.notes: This technical regulation outlines the general requirements for food to be considered Halal, which impacts ingredient selection and processing, and must be adhered to if a Halal claim is made on the label.
rules.evidenceQuote: الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية
rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for various food products that must be declared on the label.
rules.evidenceQuote: فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية
rules.notes: This standard provides guidelines for optional shelf life periods for food products, which, if chosen, must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.foodCategory: Prepackaged food stuffs, including Seasoning, Snack Food, Processed Foods
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 - Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: Specific labelling requirements for prepackaged food stuffs must be verified within the full text of GSO 9:2022.
rules.evidenceQuote: بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة (Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs)

rules.foodCategory: Halal Food, including Seasoning, Snack Food, Processed Foods (if claimed Halal)
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 - HALAL FOOD - Part 1 : General Requirements
rules.notes: Specific general requirements for Halal food must be verified within the full text of GSO 2055-1:2015.
rules.evidenceQuote: الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال (HALAL FOOD - Part 1 : General Requirements)

rules.foodCategory: Food products, including Seasoning, Snack Food, Processed Foods
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 - Expiration dates for food products - Part 1 : Mandatory expiration dates
rules.notes: Specific mandatory expiration date requirements for food products must be verified within the full text of GSO 150-1:2013.
rules.evidenceQuote: فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية (Expiration dates for food products -Part 1 : Mandatory expiration dates)

rules.foodCategory: Food products, including Seasoning, Snack Food, Processed Foods
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 - Expiration dates for food products - Part 2 : Voluntary expiration dates
rules.notes: Specific voluntary expiration date guidelines for food products must be verified within the full text of GSO 150-2:2013.
rules.evidenceQuote: فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية (Expiration dates for food products - Part 2 : Voluntary expiration dates)

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 1016:2015 - Microbiological Criteria for Foodstuffs
rules.notes: Specific microbiological limits for various foodstuffs and food ingredients must be verified within the full text of GSO 1016:2015.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Specific limits and conditions for food additives in processed foods (including seasoning, snack food) must be verified in the relevant BPOM regulations for Pangan Olahan.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.sourceTitle: SE Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)
rules.notes: Specific provisions for the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive in processed foods must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: SE Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 Surat Edaran Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Mandatory allergen declaration requirements for processed foods (including seasoning, snack food) must be verified in the relevant BPOM regulations for Pangan Olahan.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Specific limits for chemical contaminants in processed foods (including seasoning, snack food) must be verified in the relevant BPOM regulations for Pangan Olahan.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.sourceTitle: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan
rules.notes: This regulation categorizes foods, which is foundational for applying specific rules and labelling requirements for processed foods (including seasoning, snack food).
rules.evidenceQuote: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Specific labelling warnings and requirements for processed foods (including seasoning, snack food) must be verified in the relevant BPOM regulations for Pangan Olahan.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Specific limits for microbiological contaminants in processed foods (including seasoning, snack food) must be verified in the relevant BPOM regulations for Pangan Olahan.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.sourceTitle: JDIH Biro Hukum dan Organisasi - Badan POM RI
rules.notes: Specific limits for salt/sodium in processed foods (including seasoning, snack food) must be verified in the relevant BPOM regulations for Pangan Olahan.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.parameter: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives)
rules.limit: Prohibited unless MHLW specifies as unlikely to cause harm
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: This implies a positive list system for additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives (...) must not be sold, or be produced, imported, processed, used, stored, or displayed for the purpose of sale, except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.limit: Must conform to MHLW established criteria for production, processing, use, cooking, preservation methods, and standards for ingredients
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Verification against specific MHLW standards and criteria is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives (...) or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives

rules.limit: Prohibited if likely to cause harm to human health
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds.

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, or pharmaceuticals for animals
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: This refers to the Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals (...) remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health (...) must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, for the purpose of sale, or sold

rules.limit: Prohibited, unless MHLW specifies as unlikely to cause harm
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.evidenceQuote: those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Designated ingredients, etc. (MHLW-designated ingredients requiring special attention)
rules.limit: Business operators must notify authorities without delay if food containing designated ingredients causes or may cause harm
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: This implies a need to identify and potentially label such ingredients for safety.
rules.evidenceQuote: A business that handles food containing any ingredient or material which requires special attention (...) must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc. of that information without delay

rules.parameter: Imported food and additives requiring certification of food sanitation management at production site
rules.limit: Must have certificates from exporting country's governmental organization
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring certification of the conditions of food sanitation management at the production site (...) must not be imported for the purpose of sale, unless they have certificates or copies thereof attached which have been issued by a governmental organization of an exporting country

rules.parameter: Imported food and additives requiring measures to control particularly important processes
rules.limit: Must be produced/processed in MHLW-specified countries/regions/facilities that take those measures
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring measures to control particularly important processes (...) must not be imported for the purpose of sale, unless they have been produced or processed in the countries or regions, or facilities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as actually taking those measures.

rules.parameter: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc.
rules.limit: Verification required against official MHLW specifications and standards
rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: These documents contain detailed limits and requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act

rules.foodCategory: livestock meat, milk, organs, and poultry meat, organs, and products thereof
rules.parameter: Imported livestock meat, etc. with diseases/abnormalities
rules.limit: Must have certificates from exporting country's governmental organization certifying freedom from specified diseases/abnormalities, or MHLW-specified electronic data
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.evidenceQuote: The meat, milk and organs of livestock, and, the meat and organs of poultry, and, products thereof (...) must not be imported as food for the purpose of sale, unless they have certificates or their copies attached which have been issued by a governmental organization of an exporting country, and which certify that they are not the meat, milk or organs of livestock, or, the meat or organs of poultry, or, products thereof which have or are suspected to have any of the diseases listed in each item of the preceding paragraph, have any of the abnormalities listed in each item of the same paragraph, or have died

rules.limit: Prohibited if contaminated or suspected to be so and likely to cause harm
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.parameter: Compliance with regulatory limits for food additives
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Different limits apply depending on the additive and the type of food products. Refer to 'Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives'.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of an additive not currently permitted.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Check the 'List of Permitted Food Additives' or use the 'Food Additives Search tool' provided by SFA.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.parameter: Compliance with regulatory limits for contaminants
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: All food businesses must comply with these limits and also with Regulatory Limits for Food Additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.parameter: Specific contaminant limits (Microbiological, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins, Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to relevant SFA documents for specific limits on various types of contaminants.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents.

rules.foodCategory: Meat and food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Prohibited under Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hexene) hexoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-n-hexane) dienoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-2, 4-hexadiene)

rules.foodCategory: Various food commodities (including potentially seasoning/snacks if containing animal products)
rules.parameter: Veterinary drug residues (other than prohibited compounds)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: SFA has established a list of MRLs for compliance, downloadable from the SFA website.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.foodCategory: Prepacked foods (seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Applies to Ready-to-eat (RTE) food.
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.parameter: γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) produced by microbial fermentation
rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Also includes labeling requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products (cuttlefish, neritic squid, octopus, squid, takoyaki, dried shredded squid)
rules.limit: Recommended declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Voluntary labeling recommendation.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive: (1) Cephalopods and cephalopod products: cuttlefish (calamari), neritic squid (small squid and squid), octopus, squid, etc. and relevant products such as takoyaki and dried shredded squid.

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
rules.limit: Mandatory declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These are 10 of the 11 mandatory allergens requiring declaration.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive... Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish

rules.parameter: Kiwis and kiwi products (Kiwi jam, dried kiwi)
rules.limit: Recommended declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Voluntary labeling recommendation.
rules.evidenceQuote: (4) Kiwis and kiwi products thereof: Kiwi jam, dried kiwi, etc.

rules.parameter: Seeds and seed products (Sunflower seed, melon seed)
rules.limit: Recommended declaration if present as ingredient or food additive (excluding highly refined/purified sunflower seed oil)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Voluntary labeling recommendation. Highly refined or purified sunflower seed oil is excluded.
rules.evidenceQuote: (3) Seeds and seed products: Sunflower seed, melon seed, etc. However, sunflower seed oil, which is highly refined or purified from sunflower seeds, is not included.

rules.parameter: Spiral shells and Spiral shell products (escargot, mussel, clam, oyster, scallop, mytilus, meretrix lusoria, abalone, scallop sauce, scallop candy, spiral shell sauce)
rules.limit: Recommended declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Voluntary labeling recommendation.
rules.evidenceQuote: (2) Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof: escargot, mussel, clam, oyster, scallop, mytilus, meretrix lusoria, abalone, etc. and relevant products such as scallop sauce, scallop candy and spiral shell sauce.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory declaration if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits are detailed in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products
rules.notes: Specific limits are detailed in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Animal Products

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims on prepackaged foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.parameter: Precautionary allergen labeling for cross-contamination risk
rules.limit: Suggested statement: “The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or synonymous terms.
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Suggested for products with a risk of allergen cross-contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: If there is a risk of a food product being affected by allergen cross-contamination... the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.sourceTitle: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร
rules.notes: Specific limits for food additives must be verified against the relevant regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.sourceTitle: บัญชีค่าปริมาณสารพิษตกค้างสูงสุดของอาเซียน (ASEAN MRLs) (ฉบับปรับปรุง กุมภาพันธ์ 2569)

rules.sourceTitle: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.sourceTitle: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.limit: Verification required against FDA listings and CFR citations
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA
rules.notes: Manufacturers must verify the regulatory status and approved uses of food and color additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Approved Food and Color Additives and Petitions Under Review, Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Citations for Color Additives, Food Ingredients and Packaging

rules.limit: Must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173, 73, 74) or evaluated by FEMA/JECFA
rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: The 'Substances Added to Food inventory' is a partial list.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively), and flavoring substances evaluated by FEMA* and JECFA*.

rules.limit: Must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184)
rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: The 'Substances Added to Food inventory' is a partial list.
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as 'PROHIBITED' or 'PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS'.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food (labeled as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS").

rules.limit: Must be declared on the label
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is a guidance level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm CPG 500.500

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an action level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400

rules.limit: Must comply with tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509. Users must refer to the transparency tool and linked guidance documents for specific contaminant-commodity pairs.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry. For ease of reference, the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels.

rules.limit: Must meet FDA's defined standard for gluten content
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Specific compliance requirements exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods (final rule August 12, 2020).
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling, which helps consumers, especially those living with celiac disease, be confident that items labeled “gluten-free” meet a defined standard for gluten content.

rules.limit: Not required by law; should only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact after good manufacturing practices are followed and must be truthful and not misleading
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Advisory statements are not a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact...

rules.foodCategory: Human food (establishments that manufacture, process, pack, or hold)
rules.limit: Establishments must put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per 21 CFR part 117 (CGMP & PC rule)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: This rule establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA’s “Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food” rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements applicable to establishments that manufacture, process, pack, or hold human food. The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.foodCategory: All food and cosmetic products containing the additives
rules.limit: Must be declared on the label
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Identified as allergenic substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.limit: Must be declared in one of two ways: 1) In parentheses following the ingredient name, or 2) Immediately after or next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The type of tree nut, species of fish, and species of Crustacean shellfish must also be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label in one of two ways. The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient... — OR — Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is at or above the limit. Required due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
